title: alloy-cli per-family toolchain manifest v1
description: Declarative per-MCU-family host toolchain manifest consumed by alloy-cli's toolchain registry.  Lives under data/families/<family_id>.yml and ships inside the alloy-cli wheel.

schema_version.description: SemVer of the manifest schema this file targets.  Major bump is breaking.

family_id.description: Kebab-case family identifier — must match the YAML filename stem.

core.description: CPU core string (e.g. cortex-m4f, cortex-m0plus, xtensa-lx6, riscv32imac).

arch.description: Optional architecture identifier (e.g. armv7em, armv6m, xtensa, rv32imac).

extends.description: Optional parent family id whose required/recommended/optional lists this manifest inherits.

required.description: Tools the family CANNOT build/flash/debug without.

recommended.description: Tools that materially improve UX (recovery, alt flashers, serial monitors).

optional.description: Power-user tools (alternative debuggers, register-aware SVD packs, etc.).

tool.description: Canonical tool name (kebab-case for CLIs; PascalCase tolerated for vendor GUIs like STM32CubeProgrammer).

version.description: SemVer-style range (e.g. ">=14,<16") evaluated by the installer in Wave 2.

source.description: Where Wave 2 fetches the binary from.  `github:` owner+repo follow GitHub's username rules (alphanumeric + hyphens, case-preserving).  `vendor` means EULA-gated — never auto-installed; the manifest carries `install_docs` instead.

capabilities.description: Closed enum of capabilities this tool exposes; alloy maps verbs (`alloy flash`, `alloy reset`, …) to the first tool advertising the matching capability.

bundles.description: Extra binaries shipped alongside the primary tool (e.g. arm-none-eabi-gdb ships with arm-none-eabi-gcc).  The registry flattens these into the capability map so `tool_for_capability("debug")` resolves to the gcc bundle when no standalone gdb is declared.

udev_required.description: Linux-only hint: the tool needs udev rules to access USB probes without sudo.  Wave 1 only renders an info row; Wave 2 emits the rules with explicit consent.

install_docs.description: Per-OS install documentation.  REQUIRED when `source = "vendor"`; ignored otherwise.
